author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Audit of `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs::to_trailer_block` (the single
writer that produces every `Jjf-*:` line in a commit description)
identified four free-form payload categories:

- **title** ‚Äî already covered by `validate_title` (`4aa787f`, issue
  `e4e483b`). No change needed.
- **slug** ‚Äî `validate_slug` enforces `[a-z0-9-]+`. Newlines
  structurally impossible. No change needed.
- **block-reason** ‚Äî `Storage::block` already rejects `\n` and `\r`
  via `Error::Invalid` (test `block_rejects_multiline_reason`,
  `crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs:2587`). No change needed.
- **assignee** ‚Äî NOT validated. Injectable via `set_assignee`,
  `update(assignee=...)`, `claim`, or `create_issue(draft.assignee)`.
- **label** ‚Äî NOT validated. Injectable via `add_label`,
  `remove_label`, or `create_issue(draft.labels)`.
- **memory-key** ‚Äî `validate_slug`, safe.
- **memory-value** ‚Äî sanitized at writer (`\n` ‚Üí space) but bare `\r`
  passed through. Rust's `str::lines()` only splits on `\n` and
  `\r\n` so bare `\r` couldn't inject in OUR parser, but a permissive
  reader could. Hardened: writer now collapses `\r\n` / `\r` / `\n`
  uniformly to spaces.

Fixes landed in `1dce1ec`:

- New public helper `validate_no_newlines` in
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:542` ‚Äî rejects `\n` and `\r` only
  (intentionally narrower than `validate_title`; labels and
  assignees are free-form by spec, only the trailer-injection shape
  matters).
- Applied to `set_assignee`, `claim`, `update(assignee)`,
  `add_label`, `remove_label`, `create_issue(draft.assignee +
  draft.labels)`.
- Memory writer collapses every newline shape, not just `\n`.
- Defense-in-depth: `Op::to_trailer_block` gains `debug_assert!`s on
  every free-form payload so any future writer regression trips
  loudly in the test suite.

New tests in `crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`:

- `crafted_title_cannot_inject_set_status_against_victim`
- `crafted_assignee_cannot_inject_trailer`
- `crafted_label_cannot_inject_trailer`
- `trailer_block_shape_walker_finds_no_orphans_on_real_bookmark` ‚Äî
  generic invariant walker, builds a bookmark exercising every op
  kind, asserts each `Jjf-Op:` stanza carries its required sibling
  trailers and no orphan `Jjf-*` appears outside a stanza.

`cargo nextest run --workspace`: 415/415 green (was 411 before).

## Recommendation

Path A (`reject at write boundary`) over Path B (`writer-side
escaping`). Cheaper, uniform across every free-form field, no
quote-aware-vs-permissive split between jjforge and any third-party
tool that grep'd the trailer block. The asterinas migration
doesn't need multi-line titles, assignees, or labels. The trailer
block stays a structured surface ‚Äî no escape rules to design,
document, or update later.

## Confidence

high. Every writer call site that takes a free-form string has been
audited and either pre-validated, structurally constrained (hex /
enum / `[a-z0-9-]+`), or sanitized at the writer (memory value).
The `debug_assert`s in `to_trailer_block` catch new writer call
sites that forget the rule. The walker test enforces the structural
invariant on every commit on the bookmark.

## Open follow-ups

- none.
